title: 10 best herbal tinctures for uterine fibroids
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for uterine fibroids are concentrated plant extracts used to alleviate symptoms associated with fibroid growths in the uterus. These tinctures help by regulating hormonal imbalances, reducing inflammation, and promoting uterine health. The best herbal tinctures for uterine fibroids include Vitex agnus-castus, Urtica dioica, Paeonia lactiflora, Angelica sinensis, Cimicifuga racemosa, Trifolium pratense, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ginkgo biloba, Angelica archangelica, and Astragalus membranaceus. If left untreated, uterine fibroids can cause heavy menstrual bleeding, pelvic pain, and anxiety, negatively impacting daily life, work, and relationships, ultimately affecting overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barley tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. These compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of fibroid tissue, improve blood flow, and relax the uterine muscles, thereby reducing cramping and pain. Additionally, barley tinctures may also help regulate hormones and improve overall pelvic circulation, further supporting their potential in managing uterine fibroids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Barley tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with uterine fibroids, alleviating symptoms such as cramping and heavy bleeding.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in barley tincture can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by fibroid growth, promoting overall uterine health and function.

Vasodilatory properties: Barley tincture's vasodilatory effects can help relax and dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing pressure on the uterus, which may help reduce symptoms such as pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Avenanthramides: Avenanthramides are a group of phenolic compounds found in Hordeum vulgare. They have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the growth and proliferation of uterine fibroid cells.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, are present in Hordeum vulgare and have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and anti-estrogenic activities. These properties can help alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids and potentially reduce their growth.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, including isoorientin and orientin, are present in Hordeum vulgare and have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and estrogen-regulating properties. These effects can contribute to the reduction of uterine fibroid growth and alleviate associated symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Barley seeds contain a compound called avenanthramides, which may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: Barley leaves may have some medicinal properties, but there is limited research on their specific use for uterine fibroids.
Roots: Barley roots may also contain some medicinal compounds, but I couldn't find any specific evidence on their use for uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dry hordeum vulgare by cutting the stalks when they turn golden brown.
Chop 1 part of the harvested hordeum vulgare into small pieces for increased surface area.
Combine the chopped hordeum vulgare with 2 parts of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hormone-regulating properties. The bitter compounds found in dandelion root have been shown to reduce inflammation and shrink the size of fibroids, while also supporting liver detoxification and hormonal balance. Additionally, dandelion's ability to stimulate bile production can help break down excess estrogen, which is often a contributing factor to uterine fibroid growth. By addressing these underlying causes, herbal dandelion tinctures offer a natural and holistic approach to managing uterine fibroids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Dandelion tincture has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids such as pain, heaviness, and bloating.

Estrogen regulator and modulator: Dandelion contains compounds that have been found to regulate and moderate estrogen levels in the body, which may help address hormonal imbalances that contribute to the growth of uterine fibroids.

Anti-fibrotic properties: Dandelion tincture has been shown to inhibit the formation of scar tissue (fibrosis), which can occur in response to injury or inflammation, potentially reducing the size and severity of uterine fibroids.
remedy_constituents: Luteolin: Luteolin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with uterine fibroids, potentially alleviating symptoms such as pain and heavy bleeding.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of terpene, may help inhibit the growth of fibroid cells and reduce their size by inducing apoptosis (cell death) and inhibiting cell proliferation, thereby potentially alleviating symptoms of uterine fibroids.

Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpene saponin, may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with uterine fibroids, potentially alleviating symptoms such as pain and heavy bleeding, and also may have estrogen-regulating effects that can help reduce fibroid growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids.
Roots: They are used because they contain taraxasterol and other saponins that may help reduce inflammation and prevent cell growth in uterine fibroids, thereby alleviating symptoms.

Flowers: They are used for their potential to reduce estrogen levels and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes in the flowers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the early morning.
Clean and chop the harvested taraxacum officinale roots and leaves into small pieces.
Combine 1 part of chopped taraxacum officinale with 2 parts of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because of its ability to promote a healthy hormonal balance in the body. The tincture contains angelic acid, which has been shown to inhibit the production of estrogen, a hormone that can contribute to the growth and development of fibroids. Additionally, the tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as heavy bleeding and pelvic pain associated with uterine fibroids. By promoting hormonal balance and reducing inflammation, angelica tinctures provide a natural and effective approach for managing uterine fibroids.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Angelica tincture's antispasmodic property helps to relax and calm muscle spasms in the uterus, reducing symptoms such as cramps and pain associated with fibroids.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in angelica tincture can help reduce inflammation and swelling caused by fibroids, which can alleviate pressure on surrounding organs and tissues.

Estrogen-regulating property: Angelica tincture has been traditionally used to regulate estrogen levels, which may help reduce the growth and size of uterine fibroids by balancing hormonal imbalances.
remedy_constituents: Ligustilide: This sesquiterpene lactone may help alleviate uterine fibroids by inducing relaxation of the uterine muscle, reducing uterine contractions and spasms.
Butylphthalide: This sesquiterpene lactone may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with uterine fibroids.

Coumarins: These phenolic compounds may have a protective effect on the uterus, potentially preventing or reducing the growth of uterine fibroids by inhibiting cell proliferation and inducing apoptosis (cell death).
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain the highest concentration of bioactive compounds, such as angelic acid and isoangelicin, which help to shrink uterine fibroids.
Leaves: They have a high content of antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh angelica archangelica root and 50g of fresh angelica archangelica leaves in the morning.
Chop the harvested angelica archangelica root and leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Combine the chopped angelica archangelica root and leaves in a clean glass jar and cover with 500ml of 35% ethanol vodka.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every 2 days.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because they contain mucilages that soothe and calm inflammation in the uterus. The tannins present in marshmallow also help to reduce excessive bleeding associated with fibroids, while the flavonoids work to strengthen and tone the uterine walls. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, herbal marshmallow tinctures can help alleviate symptoms such as heavy bleeding, cramping, and bloating often experienced by women with uterine fibroids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow root tincture reduces inflammation and swelling associated with uterine fibroids, helping to alleviate symptoms such as pelvic pain and cramping.
Antioxidant activity: Marshmallow root's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to fibroid growth and severity.
Soothing and demulcent properties: The tincture's mucilage content helps soothe and calm irritated tissues in the uterus and surrounding areas, reducing discomfort and promoting relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Gallic acid: Helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in uterine fibroids, potentially alleviating symptoms and promoting healing.
Althaea polysaccharides: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help soothe and reduce the size of uterine fibroids, thereby alleviating discomfort and pain.
Vitexin: A flavonoid that may help regulate hormonal balance and reduce the growth of uterine fibroids by modulating estrogen and progesterone levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Althaea officinalis roots are commonly used in tinctures for uterine fibroids due to their high content of mucilages, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe the uterine lining.
Leaves: Althaea officinalis leaves are used in tinctures for uterine fibroids because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that may help to alleviate pain and reduce fibroid growth.

Flowers: Althaea officinalis flowers are used in tinctures for uterine fibroids due to their ability to regulate hormonal imbalances and promote menstrual cycle health, which may help to reduce fibroid symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of althaea officinalis in early spring or late summer when the plant is dormant.
Clean and dry the roots completely to prevent contamination and ensure proper extraction.
Combine the dried roots with 70 vodka or ethanol at a 1:5 ratio in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the container every few days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When used topically or taken internally, it reduces inflammation and swelling in the uterus, helping to alleviate symptoms such as heavy bleeding, pelvic pain, and bloating. Additionally, licorice tinctures may help regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to fibroid growth, promoting a healthy uterine environment and reducing the size and severity of fibroids over time.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal licorice tinctures contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids such as pelvic pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant: Licorice root has antioxidant compounds that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to fibroid growth and progression, and may help protect against cellular damage caused by fibroids.

Hormone-balancing: Herbal licorice tinctures contain isoflavones and other phytoestrogens that have been shown to help balance hormonal levels, including estrogen and progesterone, which can contribute to the growth and proliferation of uterine fibroids.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Glycosides: These flavonoid compounds help with uterine fibroids by potentially reducing inflammation and promoting the breakdown of fibroid tissue.
Glycyrrhetic Acid: This triterpene acid may help alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids by reducing prostaglandin production, which can contribute to menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding.
Isoliquiritigenin: This isoflavonoid may exhibit anti-inflammatory and anti-estrogenic properties, which can help reduce the growth and proliferation of uterine fibroid cells.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are commonly used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate the symptoms of uterine fibroids.
Leaves: Glycyrrhiza glabra leaves are used for their ability to stimulate uterine contractions and help regulate menstrual cycles.
Stems: Glycyrrhiza glabra stems are used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of the root of glycyrrhiza glabra on a dry day in late summer.
Chop the root into small pieces and dry them in a warm place for 2 days.
Combine 1 part of the dried root with 2 parts of 95 ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Shake the jar every day for 2 weeks to allow the roots to macerate in the ethanol.
Strain and discard the solids then store the tincture in a cool dark place for 2 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Agrimony tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate cramping associated with these growths. The herb's ability to tone the uterine muscle also helps to regulate menstrual cycles and ease symptoms such as heavy bleeding and bloating. Additionally, agrimony's natural astringent properties may help to shrink fibroids over time, promoting a healthier and more balanced reproductive system. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to support women's health and alleviate the discomfort associated with uterine fibroids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Agrimony's anti-inflammatory effects may help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with uterine fibroids, promoting a healthier uterine environment.
Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of Agrimonia tincture can help alleviate menstrual cramps, heavy bleeding, and other symptoms caused by uterine fibroids.
Tonic property: As a tonic herb, Agrimony helps regulate the uterus and female reproductive system, which may improve the overall health and function of the uterus and reduce fibroid growth.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with uterine fibroids, potentially shrinking their size.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): As potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, flavonoids help protect the uterus from oxidative damage, reduce inflammation, and inhibit the growth of fibroid cells, alleviating symptoms.

Coumarins (specifically, scopoletin and umbelliferone): These compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiproliferative effects, which may help reduce the size and number of fibroids, and alleviate associated symptoms like pain and heavy bleeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help reduce inflammation and ease uterine fibroid symptoms.

Roots: Roots are traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various gynecological issues, including uterine fibroids, due to their purported ability to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce fibroid growth.
Flowers: Agrimonia eupatoria flowers are used for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate cramps and discomfort associated with uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest agrimonia eupatoria leaves and flowers in mid-summer when they are in full bloom and dried completely.
Combine 100g of dried agrimonia eupatoria with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the container daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Label the bottle with the date, ingredients, and recommended usage, and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because it contains a unique combination of compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation, relax uterine muscles, and improve circulation. The tannins in Jesuit's bark help to shrink the size of the fibroids by reducing their blood supply, while the flavonoids and phenolic acids work to combat oxidative stress and promote healing. Additionally, the tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate symptoms such as heavy bleeding and pelvic pain associated with uterine fibroids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Jesuit's Bark contains anti-inflammatory compounds like oleanolic acid, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.
Antioxidant: The tincture has high antioxidant activity, which can help neutralize free radicals that may contribute to the development of fibroids by reducing oxidative stress in the body.

Uterine relaxant: Maytenus ilicifolia extracts have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the uterine muscle, which can help alleviate symptoms like cramping and heavy bleeding associated with fibroids.
remedy_constituents: Quinine alkaloids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with uterine fibroids, making them more manageable.
Quinic acid: This compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the growth and severity of uterine fibroids.
Quinovic acid glycoside: This constituent has been found to have antiproliferative effects, inhibiting the growth of uterine fibroid cells and potentially reducing their size.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used due to its high content of quinine, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Leaves: Utilized for their alkaloid content, particularly quinine and quinidine, which help reduce uterine cramping and alleviate symptoms.
Stems: Employed for their quinidine and quinine content, which help regulate uterine contractions and reduce pain associated with fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinchona officinalis bark at dawn when the moisture content is highest and the bark is most potent.
Dry the cinchona officinalis bark in a single layer at 60 degrees celsius for 24 hours to preserve its properties.
Combine 250 grams of dried cinchona officinalis bark with 750 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow the cinchona officinalis to infuse into the ethanol.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because it targets the hormonal imbalance that often contributes to their growth. The herb, also known as Vitex agnus-castus, is a natural progesterone regulator, which helps to normalize estrogen levels in the body. By balancing hormones, chaste tree tincture may help reduce the size and discomfort associated with uterine fibroids, alleviating symptoms such as heavy bleeding, pelvic pain, and bloating. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may also help to soothe and calm the uterine lining, further reducing fibroid-related issues.
remedy_properties: Agonist activity on progesterone receptors: Chaste tree tincture helps to balance progesterone levels, which can help reduce the growth and size of uterine fibroids.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Chaste tree's antioxidant compounds help protect the uterus from damage caused by free radicals and inflammation, which can contribute to fibroid development and growth.

Uterine relaxant and spasmodic effects: Chaste tree tincture has been shown to relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramps and spasm associated with fibroids, and helping to improve overall uterine function.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides (aglycones): These compounds help reduce uterine fibroid size and alleviate symptoms by inhibiting the growth of estrogen-dependent fibroid cells and modulating the body's hormonal balance.

Flavonoids (kaempferol, quercetin): These plant-derived compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with fibroid growth, promoting a healthier uterine environment.

Saponins (agnuside, vitexicoside): Saponins in Vitex agnus castus have been shown to exhibit anti-fibrotic properties, reducing the accumulation of extracellular matrix and promoting the regression of fibroid growth, thus alleviating symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used for their estrogen-regulating properties, which help alleviate fibroid symptoms.
Seeds: They are used to support the body's natural hormonal balance, which can help reduce fibroid growth and discomfort.
Leaves: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce fibroid-related pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part dried vitex agnus castus flowers and 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried vitex agnus castus flowers and vodka in the glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Ginkgo's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to the development and growth of uterine fibroids.

Vasodilatory and Blood Flow Promotion: Ginkgo's ability to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation may help increase blood flow to the uterus, potentially reducing symptoms such as heavy bleeding, pain, and pressure caused by fibroids.

Barks: Barks are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce fibroid size and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.

remedy_recipe: Gather 50g of ginkgo biloba leaves and 50g of vodka in a clean glass container.
Combine the ginkgo biloba leaves with 50g of glycerin in a separate container.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in the glycerin for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place.
Combine the vodka with the steeped ginkgo biloba leaves in the glass container.
Filter the tincture through cheesecloth and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried roots of echinacea angustifolia in late summer or early fall.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped roots with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because it has a long history of use in traditional Chinese medicine to regulate menstruation, reduce uterine bleeding, and alleviate symptoms associated with fibroid tumors. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried paeonia lactiflora root and cut it into small pieces weighing about one pound.
Combine the cut roots with 70 percent vodka in a clean glass jar covering them completely by at least two inches.
Store the jar in a cool dark place allowing the mixture to steep for six weeks shaking it daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and reserve the liquid.
Transfer the final product to dark glass bottles using a dropper cap and store it in the refrigerator for up to two years.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal cotton tinctures containing anti-inflammatory herbs like Chamomile, Calendula, or Plantain can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with uterine fibroids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried gossypium hirsutum flowers in bulk to ensure sufficient material.
Combine one part of the dried gossypium hirsutum flowers with three parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow for maximum extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bottle and discard the solids.
Transfer the tincture to smaller dropper bottles and label with dosage instructions of 20-30 drops three times daily.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried clematis virginiana leaves and stems, weighing approximately one ounce.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 80% proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar, filling it three-quarters full.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily for 14 days, allowing for thorough extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean dark glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the product with dosage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The essential oils present in lavender, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax the uterine muscles, thereby alleviating symptoms associated with fibroids such as cramping and heavy bleeding. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning when they are dry and free of dew for optimal potency.
Combine one part dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with two parts high-proof vodka or brandy in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily to extract essential oils and compounds from the herbs.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids after pressing the liquid gently.
Label the tincture with the date and contents, then store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years, taking 20-30 drops 3 times daily as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because of its anti-inflammatory and antifibrotic properties, which may help reduce fibroid size and alleviate symptoms. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems on a sunny day after morning dew.
Chop 1 pound of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Steep 1 pound of chopped urtica dioica leaves and stems in 2 cups of 80 proof vodka for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids from the mixture.
Transfer the liquid tincture to dark glass bottles and store in a cool dark place for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because of its potential to balance hormonal fluctuations, reduce inflammation, and promote uterine health. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh paeonia lactiflora roots and flowers by cutting them at dawn when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested paeonia lactiflora roots and flowers in a single layer at 35 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Combine 1 part dried paeonia lactiflora roots and flowers with 2 parts 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a cool dark place with occasional shaking and then strain the liquid.
Filter the resulting paeonia lactiflora tincture through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because of its unique blend of bioactive compounds, including ferulic acid, ferulic acid esters, and other phytochemicals. Clean and dry the harvested plant material to prevent contamination and preserve its medicinal properties for 24 hours.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for extraction by hand or with a tool.
Combine the chopped plant material with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1:5 by weight and let it steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the tincture and transfer it to a dark glass bottle for storage and use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because it is rich in phytoestrogens, compounds that mimic the effects of estrogen in the body. remedy_recipe: Harvest cimicifuga racemosa roots and rhizomes in early spring or fall to ensure optimal potency.
Clean and dry the roots and rhizomes for 2-3 weeks to prevent contamination and mold growth.
Cut the dried roots and rhizomes into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar.
Fill a glass jar with 1 part dried cimicifuga racemosa roots and rhizomes and 2 parts 80 proof vodka.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks then strain and discard the solids to extract the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because of its rich content of isoflavones, particularly genistein and daidzein. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried trifolium pratense flowers at peak potency and store them in a cool dry place for later use.
Combine 1 part of the dried flowers with 2 parts of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks or until the liquid is fully infused.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place and label it with the preparation date and trifolium pratense concentration.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because of its anti-inflammatory and anti-estrogenic properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of dried roots in late summer or early fall after the plant has gone to seed.
Clean the roots thoroughly with a soft brush to remove any debris or dirt.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a glass container in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 fresh or dried ginkgo biloba leaves and stems weighing about 100 grams.
Chop the ginkgo biloba material into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine the chopped ginkgo biloba with 500 milliliters of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking and stirring.

Leaves: They are often used in combination with roots to enhance the therapeutic effects, as they contain similar bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.

Stems: They may be used in some cases as an alternative to roots, particularly when roots are not available, but they are less potent and contain slightly different bioactive compounds that still offer some benefits in treating uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of fresh angelica archangelica root and 25g of fresh angelica archangelica leaves on a dry sunny morning.
Chop 10g of the harvested angelica archangelica root into small pieces and 10g of the leaves into smaller pieces.
Combine the chopped angelica archangelica root and leaves with 50% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio by volume in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every 2 days.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus tinctures helps with uterine fibroids because of its potential to enhance immune function and reduce inflammation. Traditional Chinese Medicine considers Astragalus a "qi tonic" that can balance the body's energy and promote healing. Some research suggests that Astragalus may inhibit the growth of fibroid cells and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to fibroid development. Additionally, Astragalus may also help to regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to the growth of uterine fibroids, providing a holistic approach to managing the condition.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with uterine fibroids, promoting a healthier uterine environment.

Flavonoids: As potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, flavonoids can help alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and protecting against oxidative damage.

Saponins: These bioactive compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and anti-proliferative properties, which may help regulate cell growth, reduce fibroid size, and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are primarily used due to their high concentration of saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-fibrotic effects.
Buds: The buds are used for their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with uterine fibroids.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their rich source of isoflavones, which may help to regulate hormonal balance and alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh astragalus membranaceus roots in late fall or early spring after the first frost to ensure optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested roots by air-drying them for at least two weeks in a warm dark place.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for efficient extraction.
Combine one part of the chopped roots with one part of a neutral solvent like 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for six to eight weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar every few days.
